channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 3 big predictions for AI in financial services in 2025
item.description: As we look ahead to 2025, the financial services landscape will be shaped not by AI as a standalone innovation, but by how it enables smarter, faster, and more personalized experiences. Here are three key predictions for how AI will transform the industry: 1.
item.dc:creator: Ben Borodach, Fast Company

item.title: One reason the U.S. government is so spooked by TikTok: AI
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here. The real reason the U.S. government wants to ban a ByteDance-owned TikTok TikTok could shut down in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: As Biden warns of an 'oligarchy,' Trump will be flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: The super-rich have long played a role in U.S. politics but have an unusually prominent spot in incoming President Donald Trump's new administration.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi And Ali Swenson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden issued an executive order Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: ChatGPT gets support for reminders and scheduled searches
item.description:
OpenAI has started rolling out a number of new features to ChatGPT, according to Techcrunch.
The big news is support for reminders; the feature allows users, for example, to ask ChatGPT to remind them when it’s time to renew an ID or passport.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Doro Unveils New Lineup of Feature Phones for Seniors - Expected at Retailers and Networks in January
item.description: Doro has revealed a new lineup of feature phones designed with senior users in mind, comprising three 4G-connected models. The Doro Leva series features one candy-bar style phone and two flip phones with clear audio, an emergency alert button, and 4G connectivity.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC posts 57% surge in profit thanks to the AI boom
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Taiwan computer chip maker TSMC reported Thursday that its profit in the last quarter rose 57%, buoyed by the artificial intelligence boom. The world’s biggest semiconductor manufacturer reported a net profit of 374.7 billion new Taiwan dollars ($11.4 billion), higher than expected.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump will 'find a way' to preserve TikTok, Waltz says
item.description: President-elect Trump will take action to preserve TikTok upon taking office, his incoming national security adviser said Wednesday, with a deadline looming for the app’s owner to sell or face a ban in the United States. Rep. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: 5 ways TikTok profoundly changed the culture
item.description: A lot has changed over the past six years. Seemingly every large media company now has a streaming service. An entire global pandemic happened, and continues to happen. We switched presidents, and then switched back.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Biden's big semiconductor law will ramp up US chip production -- but at a high cost, report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sweeping 2022 law, touted by President Joe Biden as a way to revive U.S. manufacturing of semiconductors and reduce the country’s reliance on foreign-made computer chips, will “sharply increase production’’ of semiconductors in the United States.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Microsoft creates new internal unit for AI development
item.description: Microsoft is creating a new engineering group focused on artificial intelligence: Core AI — Platform and Tools. The group combines the company’s Developer Division with the AI Platforms work teams and some employees who previously reported to Microsoft’s CTO.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg will cohost reception with Republican billionaires for Trump inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is cohosting a reception with billionaire Republican donors next week for Donald Trump’s inauguration, the latest sign of the Facebook founder's embrace of the president-elect.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Biden signs ambitious order to bolster energy resources for AI data centers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Joe Biden on Tuesday signed an ambitious executive order on artificial intelligence that seeks to ensure the infrastructure needed for advanced AI operations, such as large-scale data centers and new clean power facilities, can be built quickly and at scale in the United
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The journey towards a knowledge graph for generative AI
item.description: How does the journey to a knowledge graph start with unstructured data—such as text, images, and other media? The evolution of web search engines offers an instructive example, showing how knowledge can be extracted from unstructured sources and refined over time into a structured, interconnected gr
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New Brazil law restricts use of smartphones in elementary and high schools
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil's President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Monday signed a bill restricting the use of smartphones at school, following a global trend for such limitations. The move will impact students at elementary and high schools across the South American nation starting in February.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and DIARLEI RODRIGUES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Britain's leader unveils AI strategy, including vow to rip up red tape hindering AI projects
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government pledged on Monday to rip up the red tape hindering construction of data centers that underpin artificial intelligence while promoting its “pro-innovation” approach to regulating the technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Open Source Software: The Future of Enterprise Infrastructure
item.description: The shift in market preferences towards open source software is driven by its superior quality, security, and extensibility. Venture investors see this trend as an opportunity to fund category leaders in enterprise software.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI isn’t going anywhere: Prompts to make life easier
item.description: Artificial intelligence can be a helpful tool for anyone who is willing to give it a try; here are some simple prompts you can use in order to make your life easier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oracle refuses to yield JavaScript trademark, Deno Land says
item.description: JavaScript runtime provider Deno Land’s efforts to get Oracle to yield the trademark for JavaScript have hit a snag, with Oracle refusing to voluntarily withdraw the trademark, Deno Land said.
item.dc:creator: Info World
